settings.heading: Practical business support when the next move needs clearer thinking
settings.text: Remote Business Partner advisory services help small business owners clarify priorities, compare options, prepare for difficult decisions, and move forward without turning every issue into a large consulting project.

settings.heading: Choose the advisory path that matches the uncertainty
settings.text: Use the Business Health Check when the issue is broad, Business Advisor for a focused decision, Decision Desk for a deeper comparison, or The Fixer when the issue needs triage first.

settings.title: Compare Core Services
settings.text: See the full service menu, prices, intake steps, and how to choose the right starting point.

settings.title: I need to know what matters first
settings.text: Use the Business Health Check when the business feels noisy, stretched, or hard to prioritise.

settings.title: I need advice on a current decision
settings.text: Use Business Advisor for a focused advisory session, trade-off review, or practical next-step plan.

settings.title: I need to compare options properly
settings.text: Use Decision Desk when the decision needs a structured comparison, risk view, and recommendation.

settings.title: Something is stuck or messy
settings.text: Use The Fixer when the issue needs triage before scope and pricing can be confirmed.

settings.heading: Clarity before the business commits more money, time, or energy
settings.text: Advisory support is designed to make business decisions more usable. The output should help you decide what to do, what to pause, what to compare, and what needs a specialist.

settings.title: Decision framing
settings.text: A clear view of the issue, options, constraints, and commercial trade-offs.

settings.title: Priority view
settings.text: A practical read on what matters now, what can wait, and what needs escalation.

settings.title: Risk and boundary check
settings.text: A plain-language look at operational, customer, supplier, owner-capacity, and scope risks.

settings.title: Recommended next step
settings.text: A proceed, pause, compare, triage, or specialist-referral pathway based on the information provided.

settings.heading: Where a practical outside view helps
settings.text: Advisory is best when the owner needs structure before action, not a generic answer or an oversized project.

settings.title: Too many possible next steps
settings.text: The business needs a priority order before the owner commits time, budget, or attention.

settings.title: Commercial pressure is rising
settings.text: A supplier, customer, cashflow, or operating decision needs a clearer plan before it becomes urgent.

settings.title: Work is stuck between people or systems
settings.text: The issue needs triage, ownership, and a practical path rather than more back-and-forth.

settings.heading: Clear outputs before follow-on work
settings.text: The right output depends on the issue: a health-check summary, an advisory recommendation, a decision brief, a triage response, or preparation for a difficult conversation.

settings.title: Business Health Check
settings.text: Use when the issue is broad, unclear, or connected to several parts of the business.

settings.title: Business Advisor
settings.text: Use when there is a current decision or operating question that needs a focused advisory view.

settings.title: Decision Desk
settings.text: Use when options need to be compared, documented, and turned into a recommendation.

settings.title: The Fixer
settings.text: Use when the issue is stuck, messy, or hard to price without first understanding the context.

settings.heading: From business issue to practical next step
settings.text: The advisory workflow keeps the process contained so the owner can act without creating a larger project than the issue needs.

settings.title: Choose the pathway
settings.text: Start with the Core Services menu or the service page that best matches the issue.

settings.title: Complete checkout or request intake
settings.text: Paid services begin with checkout. The Fixer begins with a no-cost triage request.

settings.title: Share the business context
settings.text: Use the matching intake form to send the decision, pressure points, constraints, and documents.

settings.title: Receive practical guidance
settings.text: Use the output to proceed, pause, compare options, request scoped work, or seek specialist advice.

settings.heading: Start with the smallest service that will clarify the issue
settings.text: If you are unsure, compare the Core Services first. If the business feels generally unclear, the Business Health Check is the safest starting point.

settings.title: Core Services
settings.text: Compare Business Health Check, Business Advisor, Decision Desk, The Fixer, and dispute support.

settings.title: Business Health Check
settings.text: Use this when the problem is broad or the next step is not obvious.

settings.title: Dispute Resolution Support
settings.text: Prepare for difficult commercial conversations and clarify when specialist advice is needed.

settings.heading: Start where the decision feels least clear
settings.text: For most owners, that means comparing Core Services or starting with the Business Health Check.

settings.title: Start with Business Health Check
settings.text: Get a structured review before choosing deeper advisory or implementation support.

settings.title: Compare Core Services
settings.text: Review the service menu, prices, intake steps, and best-fit guidance.
